scan_scope: repository-wide desktop WebSocket auth boundary shard
evidence: loopback listeners authorize any chrome-extension:// or moz-extension:// Origin and skip authToken; tests explicitly assert tokenless loopback extension origins are allowed, and the default extension endpoint omits the token

method: Full-file review plus focused vitest evidence from extension background/desktop transport shard
evidence: connectionAuth.test.ts lines 20-32 assert arbitrary chrome-extension and moz-extension origins are allowed on loopback without token. Focused command passed: pnpm exec vitest run apps/desktop-app/src/main/connectionAuth.test.ts packages/contracts/src/network-endpoints.test.ts apps/desktop-app/src/main/connectionManager.test.ts apps/extension/src/background/messaging/ContentMessageRouter.test.ts.
proof_gap: No live malicious-extension WebSocket harness was run in this shard; static auth predicate and tests make the bypass condition explicit.

source: Malicious installed browser extension or client forging an extension-looking Origin on loopback
broken_control: Origin regex is not pinned to this extension id and loopback skips token validation
sink: connectionManager.handleSocketMessage accepts source usp-extension messages and remembers tab ids to the attacker socket
impact: Unauthorized desktop transport control, arbitrary tab/video state injection, subtitle workflow triggering, and receipt of later desktop control commands for registered tab ids
preconditions: Desktop listener is running on default loopback endpoint
Attacker can initiate a WebSocket handshake with Origin matching chrome-extension:// or moz-extension:// syntax
severity_facts: Crosses browser extension boundary into Electron desktop main process transport
No desktop token is needed on loopback
Ordinary web origins are rejected, so the primary source is malicious extension or origin-forging local client
